# PumpX2Kit — LLM guide

> A Swift port of the Tandem t:slim X2 / Mobi Bluetooth protocol (from jwoglom's pumpX2), used by the
> faBolus app. It builds pump messages, runs the JPAKE pairing/auth, and speaks BLE — and is verified
> **byte-exact** against a vendored Java "oracle". Safety-critical: a wrong byte can misdose insulin.
> This file orients an LLM adding a message, fixing parsing, or debugging pairing.

## The golden rule: parity with the oracle
- `vendor/pumpx2-oracle/` — the reference Java implementation + its test vectors. Every request's bytes
  and every response's parse are asserted equal to the oracle. **Never change message bytes without a
  matching oracle-parity test.** See `Tests/PumpX2MessagesTests/` (e.g. `OracleParityTests`,
  `ResponseParityTests`, `InitiateBolusExtendedTests`).
- Run tests: `./scripts/test.sh` (needs **JDK 21** for the oracle). Swift-only tests:
  `swift test --filter <Name>`.

## Products (SPM targets)
- `Sources/PumpX2Messages/` — request/response message types + framing. `Requests/…`,
  `Responses/Responses.swift`, `Core/MessageProps.swift` (per-message `signed`, `characteristic`,
  `modifiesInsulinDelivery`, opcode, size). `ResponseParser` dispatches on **(characteristic, opcode)**
  — opcodes are NOT globally unique (see the `tandem-opcode-collision` note).
- `Sources/PumpX2Auth/` — `PairingCoordinator` (client-initiated JPAKE state machine), `JpakeAuth`/
  `EcJpakeContext` (EC-JPAKE over vendored mbedTLS), `Crypto` (HMAC/HKDF). Derives the long-term
  secret + per-command signing key.
- `Sources/PumpX2BLE/` — `PumpBLEClient` (CoreBluetooth central; state restoration via
  `restoreIdentifier`; the `WritePolicy` interlock: `.readOnly`/`.allowNonDelivery`/`.allowDelivery`).
- Vendored C: mbedTLS (JPAKE crypto).

## Add / change a message — where things are
- New request: add under `Sources/PumpX2Messages/Requests/…` with correct `MessageProps` (opcode,
  size, `signed`, `characteristic`, `modifiesInsulinDelivery`, responseOpCode); register the response in
  `ResponseParser`; add a byte-exact test from an oracle vector.
- Signed/insulin-affecting messages must set `modifiesInsulinDelivery: true` and go out only under the
  right `WritePolicy` — the faBolus app relies on this interlock.

## Consumed by
- `../faBolus` (the app) via SwiftPM. App-level integration + safety layering lives there; the pump
  wire format lives here.

## Conventions
- Match the oracle exactly; every type has a doc-comment citing its Java origin. If a field's meaning is
  unverified on-device, say so in the doc-comment rather than guessing.
